# YUM4J

> Yet Useful Math for Java.

YUM4J is a high-performance Java toolkit for numerical modeling and scientific computing. It supports derivative-free optimization, unconstrained/bound-constrained optimization, equality/inequality constrained optimization, nonlinear least-squares fitting, root finding, numerical integration, ODE solving, regression, matrix decompositions, FFTs, Kalman/state-space workflows, and probability distribution APIs.

## Algorithm Selection

- Derivative-free global optimization (non-convex, noisy, no gradient) → CMA-ES (`Minimizer.cmaes()`)
- Derivative-free optimization (no gradient) → Subplex (`Minimizer.subplex()`)
- Unconstrained or bound-constrained optimization → L-BFGS-B (`Minimizer.lbfgsb()`)
- Equality or inequality constrained optimization → SLSQP (`Minimizer.slsqp()`)
- Nonlinear least-squares fitting → TRF (`Minimizer.trf()`)
- Scalar root-finding → Brentq (`RootFinder.brentq()`)
- Multivariate root-finding with Jacobian → HYBR (`RootFinder.hybr()`)
- Multivariate root-finding Jacobian-free → Broyden (`RootFinder.broyden()`)
- Numerical integration (adaptive/fixed/double-exponential/oscillatory/improper/singular/sampled/Filon) → `Integrator`
- ODE initial value problems (RK23/RK45/DOP853/BDF/Radau, dense output, events) → `Integrator.ode()`
- FFT (complex, real, DCT, DST, Hartley) → `FFT` facade (NumPy-compatible), `Transform` (pocketfft-compatible)
- Matrix decompositions → `Decomposer` facade (LU, QR, LQ, SVD, Cholesky, Schur, Eigen, GEVD, GGEVD, GSVD)
- Linear regression (OLS/WLS) → `Regressor` facade

## Package Structure

```
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim             # Minimizer, RootFinder, Bound, Optimization (result + Status)
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.cmaes       # CMAESProblem, CMAESWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.subplex     # SubplexProblem, SubplexWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.lbfgsb      # LBFGSBProblem, LBFGSBWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.slsqp       # SLSQPProblem, SLSQPWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.trf         # TRFProblem, TRFWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.fft               # FFT (user facade), Transform (low-level), FftPlanCache, FftWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.root        # BrentqProblem, HYBRProblem, HYBRWorkspace, BroydenProblem, BroydenWorkspace
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad              # Integrator, Integral, Quadrature (result + Status), Trajectory (ODE result)
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.de           # DoubleExponentialIntegral, DoubleExponentialCore, DEPool, DEOpts
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.adapt        # AdaptiveIntegral, AdaptivePool
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.gauss        # FixedIntegral, WeightedIntegral, GaussRule, GaussPool
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.gauss.rule   # JacobiRule, LegendreRule, LaguerreRule, HermiteRule, GeneralizedLaguerreRule, GeneralizedHermiteRule
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.special      # OscillatoryIntegral, OscillatoryOpts, EndpointSingularIntegral, EndpointOpts
                                         # PrincipalValueIntegral, ImproperIntegral, ImproperOpts
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.sampled      # SampledIntegral, CumulativeIntegral, SampledRule, FilonIntegral, FilonOpts
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.ode          # IVPIntegral, ODE, ODEEvent, IVPPool, IVPCore
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.ode.rk       # RungeKuttaCore, RungeKuttaPool, ButcherTableau
com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.ode.implicit # BDFCore, BDFPool, RadauCore, RadauPool, ImplicitCore, ImplicitPool
com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg            # Decomposer, Decomposition, Regression, Regressor
com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.mat        # LU, QR, LQ, SVD, Cholesky, Schur, Eigen, GEVD, GGEVD, GSVD
com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.reg        # OLS, WLS, Prediction
```

Typical import for quadrature:
```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.adapt.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.de.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.gauss.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.special.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.sampled.*;
```

Typical import for optimization:
```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.cmaes.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.subplex.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.lbfgsb.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.slsqp.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.trf.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.root.*;
```

Typical import for matrix decompositions:
```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.mat.*;
```

Typical import for linear regression:
```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.reg.*;
```

## Quick Start

### CMA-ES (Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.cmaes.*;

// Basic usage: derivative-free global optimization
Optimization r = Minimizer.cmaes()
    .objective((x, n) -> { double s = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) s += x[i]*x[i]; return s; })
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();

// sep-CMA-ES (diagonal mode, O(n) per iteration)
Optimization r2 = Minimizer.cmaes()
    .objective(fn)
    .initialPoint(x0)
    .updateMode(UpdateMode.SEP_CMA)
    .maxGenerations(500)
    .solve();

// With IPOP restart strategy and bounds
Optimization r3 = Minimizer.cmaes()
    .objective(fn)
    .initialPoint(x0)
    .initialSigma(0.5)
    .restartMode(RestartMode.ipop(9, 2))
    .maxEvaluations(100000)
    .bounds(Bound.between(-5, 5), Bound.between(-5, 5))
    .solve();
```

### Derivative-free optimization (Subplex / Nelder-Mead)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.subplex.*;

// No gradient required — works for any dimension
Optimization r = Minimizer.subplex()
    .objective((x, n) -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1])
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();

// With bounds and custom step sizes
Optimization r2 = Minimizer.subplex()
    .objective(fn)
    .initialPoint(x0)
    .bounds(Bound.between(0, 10), Bound.between(0, 10))
    .initialStep(1.0, 2.0)        // per-dimension step sizes
    .functionTolerance(1e-8)
    .maxEvaluations(50000)
    .solve();
```

### Bound-constrained optimization (L-BFGS-B)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.lbfgsb.*;

Optimization r = Minimizer.lbfgsb()
    .objective((x, n) -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1])
    .bounds(Bound.atLeast(0.5), Bound.atLeast(0.5))
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();

if (r.status().converged()) {
    System.out.println(Arrays.toString(r.solution()));
} else {
    System.out.println(r.errorMessage());
}
```

### Equality-constrained optimization (SLSQP)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.slsqp.*;

// minimize x[0] + x[1], subject to x[0]^2 + x[1]^2 = 1
Optimization r = Minimizer.slsqp()
    .objective((x, n) -> x[0] + x[1])
    .equalityConstraints((x, n) -> x[0]*x[0] + x[1]*x[1] - 1)
    .initialPoint(0.5, 0.5)
    .solve();
```

### Nonlinear least squares (TRF)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.trf.*;

Optimization r = Minimizer.trf()
    .residuals((x, n, res, m) -> {
        res[0] = x[0] - 3.0;
        res[1] = x[1] - 4.0;
    }, 2)
    .initialPoint(0.0, 0.0)
    .solve();
```

### Scalar root-finding (Brentq)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.root.*;

// Find root of sin(x) in [3, 4] → π
Optimization r = RootFinder.brentq(Math::sin)
    .bracket(Bound.between(3.0, 4.0))
    .solve();
double root = r.root(); // ≈ π
```

### Multivariate root-finding (HYBR / Broyden)

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.optim.root.*;

// HYBR (Powell hybrid, requires Jacobian — numerical by default)
Optimization r = RootFinder.hybr((x, n, f, m) -> {
        f[0] = x[0]*x[0] - 2;
        f[1] = x[1] - x[0];
    }, 2)
    .initialPoint(1.0, 1.0)
    .solve();
double[] solution = r.solution(); // [√2, √2]

// Broyden (Jacobian-free)
RootFinder.broyden(fn, 2).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).solve();

// Central-difference Jacobian (HYBR only)
RootFinder.hybr(fn, 2).jacobian(NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL).initialPoint(1.0, 1.0).solve();
```

## Core API

### Minimizer (facade — static factory entry point)

```java
Minimizer.cmaes()    // → CMAESProblem (derivative-free global, CMA-ES)
Minimizer.subplex()  // → SubplexProblem (derivative-free)
Minimizer.lbfgsb()   // → LBFGSBProblem
Minimizer.slsqp()    // → SLSQPProblem
Minimizer.trf()      // → TRFProblem
```

### CMAESProblem (CMA-ES derivative-free global optimizer)

```java
Minimizer.cmaes()
    .objective(Univariate.Objective)         // no gradient needed
    .initialPoint(double...)                 // sets x₀ and infers dimension
    .bounds(Bound...)                        // optional, lb ≤ x ≤ ub
    .initialSigma(double)                           // initial step size σ₀, default 0.3
    .populationSize(int)                     // λ; default 4 + floor(3*ln(n))
    .maxIterations(int)                      // per-run generation limit, default 1000
    .maxEvaluations(int)                     // absolute evaluation budget, default λ×1000
    .maxGenerations(int)                     // generation budget (resolved lazily as n×λ)
    .updateMode(UpdateMode)                  // ACTIVE_CMA (default) / CLASSIC_CMA / SEP_CMA
    .restartMode(RestartMode)                // null=none / RestartMode.ipop(n,k) / RestartMode.bipop(n)
    .maxResample(int)                        // max resampling attempts for infeasible points, default 0
    .stopFitness(double)                     // stop when best < value, default -∞
    .parameterTolerance(double)              // step-size convergence threshold, default 1e-11
    .functionTolerance(double)               // fitness history range threshold, default 1e-12
    .maxSigmaRatio(double)                 // σ divergence guard, default 1e3
    .random(Random)                          // RNG for reproducibility
    .solve()                                 // → Optimization
    .workspace()                             // → CMAESWorkspace (for reuse)
```

**UpdateMode** — covariance matrix update strategy:

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `ACTIVE_CMA` | Full C with Active CMA negative-weight update (default; best on ill-conditioned problems) |
| `CLASSIC_CMA` | Full C without Active CMA (classic CMA-ES) |
| `SEP_CMA` | Diagonal C only — sep-CMA-ES, O(n) per iteration, skips O(n²) allocations |

**RestartMode** — multi-start strategy (pass `null` for no restart):

```java
RestartMode.ipop(maxRestarts, popSizeMultiplier)  // IPOP: λ *= multiplier each restart
RestartMode.bipop(maxRestarts)                     // BIPOP: alternate large/small populations
```

**CMAESWorkspace memory layout** (arena-based, zero per-iteration allocation):

| Arena | Size | Contents |
|-------|------|----------|
| `nVec` | 8n | xmean · xold · p_σ · p_c · D · diagC · diagD · eigenVals (scratch slots reused) |
| `lVec` | 3λ | fitness · weights · penalty (scratch) |
| `mat` | 2n² | C · B (null in SEP_CMA mode) |

In SEP_CMA mode, `mat`, `ctmpBuf` and `eigenWork` are not allocated, reducing memory from O(n²) to O(nλ).

### SubplexProblem (Subplex derivative-free, Rowan 1990)

```java
Minimizer.subplex()
    .objective(Univariate.Objective)         // no gradient needed
    .initialPoint(double...)                 // sets x₀ and infers dimension
    .bounds(Bound...)                        // optional, lb ≤ x ≤ ub
    .initialStep(double...)                  // optional, per-dimension step sizes
    .parameterTolerance(double)              // default 1e-4
    .functionTolerance(double)               // default 1e-4
    .maxEvaluations(int)                     // default N * 200
    .solve()                                 // → Optimization
    .workspace()                             // → SubplexWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### LBFGSBProblem (L-BFGS-B bound-constrained quasi-Newton)

```java
Minimizer.lbfgsb()
    .objective(Univariate.Objective)        // auto-uses NumericalGradient.CENTRAL; signature: (x, n) -> double
    .objective(Univariate)                  // analytic gradient; signature: (x, n, g) -> double
    .initialPoint(double...)                // sets x₀ and infers dimension
    .bounds(Bound...)                       // optional, lb ≤ x ≤ ub
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 15000
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 15000
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default 1e-5
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e7 * ε
    .corrections(int)                       // L-BFGS memory parameter m, default 10
    .maxLineSearchSteps(int)                // default 20
    .solve()                                // → Optimization
    .workspace()                            // → LBFGSBWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### SLSQPProblem (SLSQP sequential least-squares programming)

```java
Minimizer.slsqp()
    .objective(Univariate.Objective / Univariate)  // Objective: (x, n) -> double; Univariate: (x, n, g) -> double
    .equalityConstraints(Univariate.Objective...)   // c(x) = 0
    .inequalityConstraints(Univariate.Objective...) // c(x) >= 0
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e-6
    .maxIterations(int)                     // default 100
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 1000
    .solve()                                // → Optimization
    .workspace()                            // → SLSQPWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### TRFProblem (TRF trust-region reflective least squares)

```java
Minimizer.trf()
    .residuals(Multivariate.Objective, int m)  // auto-uses NumericalJacobian.CENTRAL; signature: (x, n, r, m) -> void
    .objective(Multivariate)                          // analytic Jacobian; signature: (x, n, r, m, jac) -> void
    .initialPoint(double...)
    .bounds(Bound...)
    .functionTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .parameterTolerance(double)             // default 1e-8
    .gradientTolerance(double)              // default 1e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 100 * n
    .loss(RobustLoss)                       // default LINEAR
    .lossScale(double)                      // soft margin C, default 1.0
    .solve()                                // → Optimization
    .workspace()                            // → TRFWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### RootFinder (facade — static factory entry point)

```java
RootFinder.brentq(DoubleUnaryOperator f)                        // → BrentqProblem
RootFinder.hybr(Multivariate.Objective fn, int n)               // → HYBRProblem
RootFinder.broyden(Multivariate.Objective fn, int n)            // → BroydenProblem
```

### BrentqProblem (1-D Brent's method)

```java
RootFinder.brentq(DoubleUnaryOperator f)
    .bracket(Bound.between(a, b))       // required: f(a)*f(b) <= 0
    .absoluteTolerance(double)          // default 2e-12
    .relativeTolerance(double)          // default 4 * ε
    .maxIterations(int)
    .solve()                            // → Optimization
```

### HYBRProblem (N-D Powell hybrid, MINPACK hybrd)

```java
RootFinder.hybr(Multivariate.Objective fn, int n)
    .jacobian(NumericalJacobian)    // default FORWARD
    .functionTolerance(double)      // default 1.49e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 200*(n+1)
    .initialPoint(double...)        // required
    .solve()                        // → Optimization
    .workspace()                    // → HYBRWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### BroydenProblem (N-D Broyden's method, Jacobian-free)

```java
RootFinder.broyden(Multivariate.Objective fn, int n)
    .functionTolerance(double)      // default 1.49e-8
    .maxEvaluations(int)                    // default 100*(n+1)
    .initialPoint(double...)        // required
    .solve()                        // → Optimization
    .workspace()                    // → BroydenWorkspace (for reuse)
```

### Optimization

```java
result.status().converged()       // whether converged
result.root()            // 1-D root value (NaN for N-D)
result.solution()        // solution vector (null if not set)
result.cost()               // objective / residual norm value at termination
result.iterations()      // iteration count
result.evaluations()     // function evaluation count
result.summary()         // single-line summary string
result.errorMessage()    // reason + suggestion on failure, null otherwise
result.toString()           // multi-line structured diagnostic output
```

### Bound

```java
Bound.unbounded()           // x ∈ (-∞, +∞), no constraint
Bound.between(lower, upper) // lower ≤ x ≤ upper
Bound.atLeast(lower)        // x ≥ lower
Bound.atMost(upper)         // x ≤ upper
Bound.exactly(value)        // x = value (fixed variable)
Bound.nonNegative()         // x ≥ 0
Bound.nonPositive()         // x ≤ 0
new Bound(lower, upper)     // public constructor; use Double.NaN for no bound on that side (for serialization / reflection)
```

### NumericalGradient

| Method | Accuracy | Evals/dim |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| `FORWARD` | O(h) | 1 |
| `BACKWARD` | O(h) | 1 |
| `CENTRAL` | O(h²) — default | 2 |
| `FIVE_POINT` | O(h⁴) | 4 |

### NumericalJacobian

| Method | Accuracy | Evals |
|--------|----------|-------|
| `FORWARD` | O(h) | n+1 |
| `CENTRAL` | O(h²) — default | 2n |

## Regressor (Linear Regression)

`com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.Regressor`

Facade for ordinary and weighted least squares regression. Solves y = Xβ + ε with optional per-observation weights. X is row-major n×k; **both OLS and WLS overwrite X in-place**. y is never modified. WLS additionally writes whitened y~ into `WLS.Pool.yWhiten`.

### Factory Methods

```java
Regressor.ols(y, X, n, k, Opts...)           // OLS — must specify Opts.QR or Opts.PINV
Regressor.ols(y, X, n, k, Pool, Opts...)     // OLS with workspace reuse
Regressor.wls(y, X, w, n, k, Opts...)        // WLS
Regressor.wls(y, X, w, n, k, Pool, Opts...)  // WLS with workspace reuse
```

### Opts

| Opt | Description |
|---|---|
| `Opts.QR` | QR factorization — faster when X is full rank |
| `Opts.PINV` | SVD pseudoinverse — robust, handles rank-deficient X |
| `Opts.HAS_CONST` | Declare X has a constant column; sets kConst=1, skips detection |
| `Opts.NO_CONST` | Declare X has no constant column; sets kConst=0, skips detection |

### Regression Result Methods (base of OLS / WLS)

```java
// Parameters
double[]   params()          // β̂, length k
double[]   bse()                 // standard errors √diag(Cov(β̂))
double[]   paramCov()            // Cov(β̂) = σ̂²·(XᵀX)⁻¹, k×k row-major

// Fit quality
double     ssr()                 // sum of squared (whitened) residuals
double     tss(boolean centered) // total sum of squares
double     ess()                 // explained sum of squares
double     scale()               // σ̂² = SSR / (n − rank)
double     r2(boolean adjusted)  // R² or adjusted R²
double[]   mse()                 // {MSE_model, MSE_residual, MSE_total}

// Information criteria
double     logLike()             // Gaussian log-likelihood
double     aic()                 // AIC = −2·llf + 2·(dfModel + kConst)
double     bic()                 // BIC = −2·llf + log(n)·(dfModel + kConst)

// Fitted values and residuals
double[]   fitted(boolean whiten)   // ŷ = Xβ̂; whitened = √W·ŷ for WLS
double[]   residual(boolean whiten) // e = y − ŷ; whitened = √W·e for WLS

// Prediction
Prediction predict(double[] newX, int m, double[] weights)

// Dimensions and diagnostics
int        nObs()                // n
int        nParams()             // k
int        kConst()              // 1 if intercept detected, 0 otherwise
int        rank()                // numerical rank of X
double     condNum()             // condition number √(σ_max²/σ_min²)
```

### Prediction

```java
Prediction pred = r.predict(newX, m, weights);
pred.mean()          // ŷ = newX·β̂, length m
pred.paramVar()      // xᵢᵀ·Cov(β̂)·xᵢ per observation
pred.residualVar()   // σ̂²/wᵢ per observation

double[][] ci = pred.confInt(0.05);  // 95% prediction interval
// ci[0] = lower bounds, ci[1] = upper bounds, ci[2] = std errors
```

### Pool (Workspace Reuse)

```java
OLS.Pool ws = new OLS.Pool();   // for OLS
WLS.Pool ws = new WLS.Pool();   // for WLS (includes yWhiten buffer)
// reuse across multiple fits of the same or similar dimensions
OLS r1 = Regressor.ols(y1, X1, n, k, ws, Regressor.Opts.PINV);
OLS r2 = Regressor.ols(y2, X2, n, k, ws, Regressor.Opts.PINV);
```

### Quick Example

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.*;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.reg.*;

// Build X = [x, 1] row-major n×2
double[] X = new double[n * 2];
for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { X[i*2] = xVec[i]; X[i*2+1] = 1.0; }

// OLS fit
OLS r = Regressor.ols(y, X, n, 2, Regressor.Opts.PINV);
double[] beta = r.params();   // [slope, intercept]
double   r2   = r.r2(false);
double   aic  = r.aic();

// WLS fit (inverse-variance weights, X is overwritten in-place)
WLS rw = Regressor.wls(y, X.clone(), weights, n, 2, Regressor.Opts.PINV);

// Prediction interval
Prediction pred = r.predict(X, n, null);
double[][] ci   = pred.confInt(0.05);
```

## Decomposer (Matrix Decompositions)

`com.curioloop.yum4j.linalg.Decomposer`

Unified facade for all matrix decompositions. All methods accept an optional `Pool` for workspace reuse and optional `Opts` enums. Input matrices are overwritten in place (row-major `double[]`).

### Decomposition Selection

| Problem | Class | Entry point |
|---|---|---|
| Solve Ax=b, inverse, determinant | `LU` | `Decomposer.lu(A, n)` |
| Least squares, rank, solve overdetermined | `QR` | `Decomposer.qr(A, m, n)` |
| Minimum-norm solution (m≤n) | `LQ` | `Decomposer.lq(A, m, n)` |
| Singular values, low-rank approx, pseudoinverse | `SVD` | `Decomposer.svd(A, m, n)` |
| Symmetric positive-definite solve | `Cholesky` | `Decomposer.cholesky(A, n)` |
| Eigenvalues of general or symmetric matrix | `Eigen` | `Decomposer.eigen(A, n)` |
| Schur form, Lyapunov equations | `Schur` | `Decomposer.schur(A, n)` |
| Generalized eigen, A sym + B sym pos-def | `GEVD` | `Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n)` |
| Generalized eigen, A and B general | `GGEVD` | `Decomposer.ggevd(A, B, n)` |
| Generalized SVD of two matrices | `GSVD` | `Decomposer.gsvd(A, m, n, B, p)` |

### Quick Reference

```java
// LU — solve, inverse, determinant
LU lu = Decomposer.lu(A, n);
lu.solve(b, x);          // solve Ax = b
lu.inverse(Ainv);        // compute A⁻¹
lu.determinant();        // det(A)
lu.cond();               // condition number
lu.toL(); lu.toU(); lu.toP();

// QR — least squares, rank-revealing
QR qr = Decomposer.qr(A, m, n);                        // standard
QR qrp = Decomposer.qr(A, m, n, QR.Opts.PIVOTING);     // rank-revealing
qr.solve(b, x);          // solve square system
qr.leastSquares(b, x);   // min‖Ax-b‖
qr.rank();               // numerical rank (pivoting mode)
qr.toQ(); qr.toR(); qr.toP();

// SVD — singular values, pseudoinverse
SVD svd = Decomposer.svd(A, m, n);                      // thin U and Vᵀ
SVD svdF = Decomposer.svd(A, m, n, SVD.Opts.FULL_U, SVD.Opts.FULL_V);
svd.singularValues();    // double[] s
svd.solve(b, x);         // pseudoinverse solve
svd.rank(); svd.cond();
svd.toU(); svd.toVT();

// Cholesky — symmetric positive-definite
Cholesky ch = Decomposer.cholesky(A, n);                // standard
Cholesky ldl = Decomposer.cholesky(A, n, Cholesky.Opts.PIVOTING); // LDLᵀ
ch.solve(b, x);
ch.determinant(); ch.logDet(); ch.cond();
ch.toL(); ldl.toD();     // D only for LDLᵀ

// Eigen — symmetric or general
Eigen es = Decomposer.eigen(A, n, Eigen.Opts.SYMMETRIC_LOWER);
Eigen eg = Decomposer.eigen(A, n);                      // general, right vectors
Eigen el = Decomposer.eigen(A, n, Eigen.Opts.WANT_LEFT, Eigen.Opts.WANT_RIGHT);
es.eigenvalues();        // double[] (real, ascending for symmetric)
eg.eigenvalues();        // double[] real parts; eigenvaluesImag() for imaginary
es.toV();                // n×n eigenvector matrix
es.toS();                // n×1 eigenvalue matrix (complex for general)

// Schur — A = Z·T·Zᵀ, Lyapunov solvers
Schur sc = Decomposer.schur(A, n);
Schur scLHP = Decomposer.schur(A, n, Schur.Opts.SORT_LHP); // sort stable eigenvalues first
sc.toT(); sc.toZ(); sc.toS();
sc.lyapunov(Q);          // AX + XAᵀ = Q
sc.discreteLyapunov(A, Q);  // AXAᵀ - X = Q

// GEVD — generalized symmetric-definite: A·x = λ·B·x
// Requires A symmetric, B symmetric positive-definite
GEVD gv = Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n);                     // type 1, lower triangle
GEVD gv2 = Decomposer.gevd(A, B, n, GEVD.Opts.UPPER, GEVD.Opts.TYPE2);
gv.eigenvalues();        // double[] (real, ascending)
gv.toV();                // n×n eigenvector matrix
gv.toS();                // n×1 eigenvalue matrix

// GGEVD — generalized non-symmetric: A·x = λ·B·x
// A and B are general matrices; eigenvalues may be complex
GGEVD gg = Decomposer.ggevd(A, B, n);
gg.alphar(); gg.alphai(); gg.beta();  // eigenvalue = (alphar+i*alphai)/beta
gg.eigenvalues();        // double[2n] interleaved (re,im) pairs
gg.toS();                // n×1 complex matrix
gg.toVR(); gg.toVL();    // right/left eigenvector matrices

// GSVD — generalized SVD of A (m×n) and B (p×n)
GSVD gs = Decomposer.gsvd(A, m, n, B, p);
gs.initialSigma();              // generalized singular values
gs.toU(); gs.toV(); gs.toQ();
gs.rank(); gs.cond();

// Workspace reuse
LU.Pool ws = Decomposer.lu(A, n).pool();
for (double[] mat : matrices) {
    LU result = Decomposer.lu(mat, n, ws);
}
```

## Integrator (Numerical Integration)

`com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.Integrator`

Unified facade for one-dimensional numerical integration. All factory methods return a builder implementing `Integral<R, W>` with `integrate()` / `integrate(workspace)` lifecycle.

### Factory Methods

```java
Integrator.fixed()                        // → FixedIntegral
Integrator.weighted()                     // → WeightedIntegral
Integrator.adaptive()                     // → AdaptiveIntegral (GK15 or Gauss-Lobatto)
Integrator.doubleExponential(DEOpts)      // → DoubleExponentialIntegral
Integrator.oscillatory(OscillatoryOpts)   // → OscillatoryIntegral
Integrator.principalValue()               // → PrincipalValueIntegral
Integrator.endpointSingular(EndpointOpts) // → EndpointSingularIntegral
Integrator.improper(ImproperOpts)         // → ImproperIntegral.Adaptive
Integrator.improperFixed(ImproperOpts)    // → ImproperIntegral.Fixed
Integrator.sampled(SampledRule)           // → SampledIntegral
Integrator.cumulative(SampledRule)        // → CumulativeIntegral
Integrator.filon(FilonOpts)               // → FilonIntegral
Integrator.ode(IVPMethod)                // → IVPIntegral (ODE IVP solver)
```

### FixedIntegral — Gauss-Legendre on [a, b]

```java
Integrator.fixed()
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)   // required
    .bounds(double min, double max)  // required, finite
    .points(int n)                   // required, exact for degree ≤ 2n-1
    .integrate()                     // → Double
    .integrate(GaussPool)            // with workspace reuse
```

### WeightedIntegral — rule's natural domain

```java
Integrator.weighted()
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)   // required
    .points(int n)                   // required
    .rule(GaussRule)                 // required: GaussRule.legendre()/laguerre()/hermite() or GaussRule.jacobi(α,β)/laguerre(s)/hermite(s)
    .integrate()                     // → Double
    .integrate(GaussPool)
```

### AdaptiveIntegral — adaptive GK15 on [a, b]

```java
Integrator.adaptive()
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)   // required
    .bounds(double min, double max)  // required
    .tolerances(double abs, double rel)  // required, at least one > 0; default 1e-10
    .breakpoints(double...)          // optional, split domain at discontinuities
    .maxSubdivisions(int)            // default 256
    .maxEvaluations(int)             // default 16384
    .rule(AdaptiveRule)              // optional: GK15 (default) or GAUSS_LOBATTO
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature
    .integrate(AdaptivePool)
```

### DoubleExponentialIntegral — Boost-style tanh-sinh / exp-sinh / sinh-sinh

```java
Integrator.doubleExponential(DEOpts opts)
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)        // required for all rules
    .function(DoubleBinaryOperator)       // TANH_SINH only; callback receives (x, complement)
    .bounds(double lower, double upper)   // required for TANH_SINH / EXP_SINH; omitted for SINH_SINH
    .tolerance(double tol)                // optional, default √ulp(1.0)
    .maxRefinements(int n)                // optional; TANH_SINH default 15, half-line rules default 9
    .minComplement(double value)          // TANH_SINH only; optional endpoint-distance floor
    .integrate()                          // → Quadrature
    .integrate(DEPool)                    // workspace reuse across repeated DE calls
```

`DEOpts.TANH_SINH` handles finite intervals and mapped infinite bounds, `DEOpts.EXP_SINH`
handles half-lines, and `DEOpts.SINH_SINH` targets the whole real line.

```java
Quadrature r = Integrator.doubleExponential(DEOpts.TANH_SINH)
    .function(Math::sin).bounds(0.0, Math.PI).tolerance(1e-10).integrate();

DoubleExponentialIntegral de = Integrator.doubleExponential(DEOpts.EXP_SINH)
    .function(x -> Math.exp(-x)).bounds(0.0, Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY);
DEPool ws = new DEPool();
Quadrature a = de.integrate(ws);
Quadrature b = de.bounds(1.0, Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY).integrate(ws);
```

### OscillatoryIntegral — ∫ f(x)·w(ω·x) dx

```java
Integrator.oscillatory(OscillatoryOpts opts)  // COS/SIN (finite) or COS_UPPER/SIN_UPPER (semi-∞)
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)
    .lowerBound(double)              // required for all opts
    .upperBound(double)              // required for COS/SIN (finite interval)
    .omega(double)                   // required, angular frequency; 0 is allowed
    .tolerances(double abs, double rel)
    .maxCycles(int)                  // semi-infinite only, default 64
    .maxSubdivisions(int)            // default 256
    .maxEvaluations(int)             // default 16384
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature
    .integrate(AdaptivePool)
```

### PrincipalValueIntegral — Cauchy P.V. ∫ f(x)/(x-c) dx

```java
Integrator.principalValue()
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)
    .bounds(double min, double max)
    .pole(double c)                  // must be finite, must not equal min or max
    .tolerances(double abs, double rel)
    .maxSubdivisions(int)
    .maxEvaluations(int)
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature
    .integrate(AdaptivePool)
```

### EndpointSingularIntegral — ∫ (x-a)^α (b-x)^β · log-factor · f(x) dx

```java
Integrator.endpointSingular(EndpointOpts opts) // ALGEBRAIC/LOG_LEFT/LOG_RIGHT/LOG_BOTH
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)
    .bounds(double min, double max)
    .exponents(double alpha, double beta)  // both > -1
    .tolerances(double abs, double rel)
    .maxRefinements(int)             // default 12
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature
    .integrate(GaussPool)
```

### ImproperIntegral — semi-infinite or infinite domain

```java
// Adaptive (with error estimate)
Integrator.improper(ImproperOpts opts)   // UPPER/LOWER/WHOLE_LINE
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)
    .lowerBound(double)              // UPPER only
    .upperBound(double)              // LOWER only
    .tolerances(double abs, double rel)
    .maxSubdivisions(int)
    .maxEvaluations(int)
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature
    .integrate(AdaptivePool)

// Fixed-point (fast, no error estimate)
Integrator.improperFixed(ImproperOpts opts)
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator)
    .lowerBound(double)              // UPPER only
    .upperBound(double)              // LOWER only
    .points(int n)                   // required
    .integrate()                     // → Quadrature (estimatedError = 0)
    .integrate(GaussPool)
```

### SampledIntegral / CumulativeIntegral

```java
// Scalar total — from pre-sampled data
Integrator.sampled(SampledRule rule)     // TRAPEZOIDAL / SIMPSON / ROMBERG
    .samples(double[] y)                 // equally spaced, dx=1
    .samples(double[] y, double dx)      // equally spaced
    .samples(double[] x, double[] y)     // explicit coordinates (strictly increasing)
    .integrate()                         // → Double

// Scalar total — from function (zero allocation for TRAPEZOIDAL and SIMPSON)
Integrator.sampled(SampledRule rule)
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator f, int n, double a, double b)
    .integrate()                         // → Double

// Running total array (result[0] == 0 always)
Integrator.cumulative(SampledRule rule)  // TRAPEZOIDAL / SIMPSON (ROMBERG not supported)
    .samples(...)
    .integrate()                         // → double[]
```

### FilonIntegral — highly oscillatory ∫ f(x)·cos(t·x) dx or ∫ f(x)·sin(t·x) dx

```java
Integrator.filon(FilonOpts opts)         // COS or SIN
    .function(DoubleUnaryOperator f)     // required
    .bounds(double a, double b)          // required, finite
    .frequency(double t)                 // required, t > 0
    .intervals(int n)                    // required, even, >= 2; integrand evaluated at n+1 points
    .integrate()                         // → Double
```

Filon's method approximates f(x) by a piecewise quadratic and integrates the product with the
trigonometric factor analytically (Abramowitz & Stegun §25.4.62–63). Far more efficient than
adaptive GK15 when t is large (high-frequency oscillation).

### Quadrature (result type)

```java
r.value()           // integral estimate
r.estimatedError()  // absolute error bound (0 for fixed-point methods)
r.status()          // Quadrature.Status enum
r.status().converged()       // true if Status.CONVERGED
r.iterations()      // adaptive subdivisions or refinement levels
r.evaluations()     // total function evaluations
r.summary()         // single-line summary string
```

**Quadrature.Status values**: `CONVERGED`, `MAX_SUBDIVISIONS_REACHED`, `MAX_EVALUATIONS_REACHED`, `MAX_CYCLES_REACHED`, `MAX_REFINEMENTS_REACHED`, `ROUND_OFF_DETECTED`, `ABNORMAL_TERMINATION`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`

### GaussRule constants

```java
GaussRule.legendre()   // ∫₋₁^1 f(x) dx
GaussRule.hermite()    // ∫₋∞^∞ e^{-x²} f(x) dx
GaussRule.laguerre()   // ∫₀^∞ e^{-x} f(x) dx
GaussRule.jacobi(α, β) // ∫₋₁^1 (1-x)^α (1+x)^β f(x) dx,  α,β > -1
```

### AdaptiveRule

```java
AdaptiveRule.GK15           // 15-point Gauss-Kronrod, exact degree 29 (default)
AdaptiveRule.GAUSS_LOBATTO  // 4-point Gauss-Lobatto, exact degree 5, endpoint reuse
```

### Workspace reuse

```java
// Reuse AdaptivePool across repeated adaptive calls
AdaptiveIntegral problem = Integrator.adaptive()
    .function(Math::sin).bounds(0.0, Math.PI).tolerances(1e-10, 1e-10);
AdaptivePool ws = new AdaptivePool();
Quadrature r1 = problem.integrate(ws);
Quadrature r2 = problem.bounds(0.0, 2.0).integrate(ws);  // ws reused

// Reuse DEPool across repeated double-exponential calls
DoubleExponentialIntegral de = Integrator.doubleExponential(DEOpts.TANH_SINH)
    .function(Math::sin).bounds(0.0, Math.PI).tolerance(1e-10);
DEPool deWs = new DEPool();
Quadrature r3 = de.integrate(deWs);
Quadrature r4 = de.bounds(0.0, 2.0).integrate(deWs);

// Reuse GaussPool across fixed-point calls
GaussPool gws = new GaussPool();
double v1 = Integrator.fixed().function(f1).bounds(0,1).points(8).integrate(gws);
double v2 = Integrator.fixed().function(f2).bounds(0,1).points(8).integrate(gws);
```

## IVPIntegral — ODE Initial Value Problem Solver

`com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.ode.IVPIntegral`

Solves dy/dt = f(t, y), y(t₀) = y₀ using adaptive step-size methods. Entry point: `Integrator.ode(IVPMethod)`.

### Constructor / Factory

```java
new IVPIntegral(IVPMethod method)          // direct construction
Integrator.ode(IVPMethod method)           // via Integrator facade
```

### Builder Methods

```java
.equation(ODE.Equation f)       // lambda: (t, y, dydt) -> void
.equation(ODE ode)              // with analytic Jacobian: (t, y, dydt, jac) -> void
.bounds(double t0, double tf)   // integration interval; tf < t0 for backward
.initialState(double... y0)     // initial condition
.tolerances(double rtol, double atol)  // default 1e-3, 1e-6
.maxStep(double h)              // default Double.MAX_VALUE
.firstStep(double h)            // default NaN (auto-estimated)
.evalAt(double[] ts)            // output at specific times only
.denseOutput(boolean b)         // enable piecewise polynomial interpolant
.detectors(ODEEvent... events)  // event detection
.integrate()                    // → Trajectory
.integrate(IVPPool workspace)   // with workspace reuse
```

### IVPMethod

| Method | Type | Order | Use case |
|--------|------|-------|----------|
| `RK23` | Explicit | 3(2) | Non-stiff, loose tolerances |
| `RK45` | Explicit | 5(4) | Non-stiff, general purpose (default) |
| `DOP853` | Explicit | 8(5,3) | Non-stiff, tight tolerances |
| `BDF` | Implicit | 1–5 | Stiff problems |
| `Radau` | Implicit | 5 | Stiff problems, high accuracy |

### Workspace Reuse

```java
IVPPool ws = IVPIntegral.workspace(IVPMethod.RK45);
for (double[] y0 : initialConditions) {
    Trajectory r = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.RK45)
        .equation(fn).bounds(0.0, 1.0).initialState(y0).integrate(ws);
}
```

### Trajectory (result type)

```java
sol.timeSeries().t()          // double[] time points, length m
sol.timeSeries().y()          // double[] state, column-major: y[i*m+j] = equation i at time j
sol.timeSeries().dim()        // number of equations n
sol.denseOutput()         // Trajectory.DenseOutput, null if not requested
sol.denseOutput().interpolate(t, out)  // evaluate at arbitrary t ∈ [t0,tf], zero allocation
sol.events()              // Trajectory.EventPoint[][], null if no detectors
sol.events()[i][j].t()    // time of j-th occurrence of i-th event
sol.events()[i][j].y()    // state at that event, length n
sol.status()           // Trajectory.Status: SUCCESS / EVENT / FAILED
!sol.status().error()  // true unless FAILED
sol.functionEvaluations()
sol.jacobianEvaluations()  // 0 for explicit methods
sol.luDecompositions()     // 0 for explicit methods
```

### ODEEvent

```java
new ODEEvent(
    (t, y) -> y[0],              // indicator g(t,y); event fires on sign change
    ODEEvent.Trigger.FALLING,    // RISING / FALLING / EITHER
    1                            // terminal count: stop after N occurrences; 0 = non-terminal
)
```

### Quick Examples

```java
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.Trajectory;
import com.curioloop.yum4j.quad.ode.*;

// Non-stiff: dy/dt = -y
Trajectory sol = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.RK45)
    .equation((t, y, dydt) -> dydt[0] = -y[0])
    .bounds(0.0, 5.0).initialState(1.0).integrate();

// Stiff: Van der Pol (BDF)
Trajectory stiff = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.BDF)
    .equation((t, y, dydt) -> {
        dydt[0] = y[1];
        dydt[1] = 1000 * (1 - y[0]*y[0]) * y[1] - y[0];
    })
    .bounds(0.0, 500.0).initialState(2.0, 0.0).tolerances(1e-3, 1e-6).integrate();

// Analytic Jacobian (faster for stiff)
ODE sys = (t, y, dydt, jac) -> {
    dydt[0] = -y[0];
    if (jac != null) jac[0] = -1.0;
};
Trajectory sol2 = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.Radau).equation(sys)
    .bounds(0.0, 5.0).initialState(1.0).integrate();

// Dense output
Trajectory dense = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.RK45)
    .equation((t, y, dydt) -> dydt[0] = -y[0])
    .bounds(0.0, 5.0).initialState(1.0).denseOutput(true).integrate();
double[] out = new double[1];
dense.denseOutput().interpolate(2.5, out);  // y(2.5)

// Event detection: stop when y[0] hits zero (falling)
Trajectory event = Integrator.ode(IVPMethod.RK45)
    .equation((t, y, dydt) -> { dydt[0] = y[1]; dydt[1] = -9.8; })
    .bounds(0.0, 100.0).initialState(0.0, 50.0)
    .detectors(new ODEEvent((t, y) -> y[0], ODEEvent.Trigger.FALLING, 1))
    .integrate();
// event.status() == Trajectory.Status.EVENT
// event.events()[0][0].t()  — precise event time
```

## Documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): Full API reference (for deep AI understanding)
